enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences with the correct modal: can, may, must.

questions.texte: You ______ greet people politely when you meet them for the first time.
questions.reponse: must

questions.texte: ______ I introduce myself to the class?
questions.reponse: May

questions.texte: Everyone ______ respect each other in a community.
questions.reponse: must

questions.texte: You ______ call me by my first name if you want.
questions.reponse: can

questions.texte: ______ I ask you a question about your culture?
questions.reponse: May

questions.texte: You ______ not interrupt people when they are speaking.
questions.reponse: must

questions.texte: ______ you help me understand this tradition?
questions.reponse: Can

enonce.texte:
Read the following dialogue about meeting and introducing people and answer the questions below.

Meeting New People

Paul: Hello, my name is Paul. What is your name?

Marie: Hi Paul. I'm Marie. Nice to meet you!

Paul: Nice to meet you too, Marie. Where are you from?

Marie: I'm from Yaoundé, Cameroon. And you?

Paul: I'm from Douala. Can I introduce you to my friend? His name is John.

Marie: Of course! I would love to meet him.

Paul: John, this is Marie. She is from Yaoundé. Marie, this is John.

John: Hello Marie. It's a pleasure to meet you.

Marie: The pleasure is mine, John. How are you?

John: I'm very well, thank you. And you?

Marie: I'm fine too. I'm happy to meet new people.

questions.texte: Where is Marie from?
questions.reponse: Marie is from Yaoundé, Cameroon.

questions.texte: Who introduces Marie to John?
questions.reponse: Paul introduces Marie to John.

questions.texte: What polite expressions are used in the dialogue?
questions.reponse: The polite expressions used include: 'Hello', 'Nice to meet you', 'The pleasure is mine', 'How are you?', 'I'm very well, thank you', 'I'm happy to meet new people'.

questions.texte: Why do you think it is important to introduce people properly?
questions.reponse: It is important to introduce people properly because it shows respect, helps people feel comfortable, and creates a positive atmosphere for communication.

enonce.texte: Identify and correct the errors in the use of modals in the following sentences.

questions.texte: You can be polite when you meet someone for the first time.
questions.reponse: Corrected: You must be polite when you meet someone for the first time. (obligation, not possibility)

questions.texte: May I to introduce myself?
questions.reponse: Corrected: May I introduce myself? (No 'to' after 'may')

questions.texte: You must to respect elders in our culture.
questions.reponse: Corrected: You must respect elders in our culture. (No 'to' after 'must')

questions.texte: Can I asks you a question?
questions.reponse: Corrected: Can I ask you a question? (Base form of verb after modal)

intitule: Creating a Dialogue for Introducing People

enonce.texte:
Create a dialogue (8-10 exchanges) between two friends who are introducing each other to new people at a community event. In your dialogue, include:

  • At least three modals (can, may, must)
  • Polite expressions for greeting and introducing
  • Questions about where people are from

questions.texte: Write the dialogue between the friends.
questions.reponse:
Sarah: Hello, my name is Sarah. What is your name?

James: Hi Sarah. I'm James. Nice to meet you.

Sarah: Nice to meet you too, James. May I ask where you are from?

James: Of course! I'm from Buea, Cameroon. And you?

Sarah: I'm from Douala. Can I introduce you to my friend? Her name is Grace.

James: Yes, I would love to meet her.

Sarah: Grace, this is James. He is from Buea. James, this is Grace.

Grace: Hello James. It's a pleasure to meet you.

James: The pleasure is mine, Grace. How are you?

Grace: I'm fine, thank you. You must tell me more about your community in Buea.

enonce.texte:
Write a short paragraph (80-100 words) about the importance of meeting and introducing people in your community. In your paragraph, include:

  • Why it is important to meet new people
  • How to introduce people properly
  • At least two modals (can, may, must)
  • Examples of polite expressions

questions.texte: Write the paragraph about interpersonal relationships.
questions.reponse:
The Importance of Meeting People

Meeting and introducing people is very important in our community. When we meet new people, we can learn about their culture and traditions. It is also a good way to make new friends. When we introduce people, we must be polite and friendly. We should say 'Nice to meet you' and ask questions about them. A good introduction helps people feel comfortable and respected. It also helps create a friendly atmosphere where everyone can communicate well. In our community, we value relationships, and introducing people is a sign of respect.

enonce.texte: Complete the following questions and answers about interpersonal relationships using the correct modals and polite expressions.

questions.texte:
Question: ______ I ask you a question about your community?
Answer: Yes, you ______. Please go ahead.
questions.reponse:
Question: May I ask you a question about your community?
Answer: Yes, you may. Please go ahead.

questions.texte:
Question: Where ______ I find information about local customs?
Answer: You ______ ask the village chief. He knows everything.
questions.reponse:
Question: Where can I find information about local customs?
Answer: You can ask the village chief. He knows everything.

questions.texte:
Question: ______ we respect people from other cultures?
Answer: Yes, we ______. We must respect everyone.
questions.reponse:
Question: Must we respect people from other cultures?
Answer: Yes, we must. We must respect everyone.

questions.texte:
Question: ______ you introduce me to your family?
Answer: Yes, I ______. I will introduce you to them.
questions.reponse:
Question: Can you introduce me to your family?
Answer: Yes, I can. I will introduce you to them.

enonce.texte:
Imagine you are at a school event. Write a short script for a role-play where you introduce two people to each other. Your role-play should include:

  • Polite greetings
  • Questions about each person
  • At least three modals (can, may, must)
  • How to end the conversation politely

questions.texte: Write the role-play script.
questions.reponse:
Alice: Hello, I'm Alice. What is your name?

Peter: Hi Alice. I'm Peter. Nice to meet you.

Alice: Nice to meet you too, Peter. May I ask what class you are in?

Peter: I'm in Form 5. And you?

Alice: I'm in Form 5 too. Can I introduce you to my friend? Her name is Grace.

Peter: Sure. I would like to meet her.

Alice: Grace, this is Peter. He is in Form 5. Peter, this is Grace.

Grace: Hello Peter. It's a pleasure to meet you.

Peter: The pleasure is mine, Grace. How are you?

Grace: I'm fine, thank you. How about you?

Peter: I'm very well, thank you. I must say this is a wonderful school event.

Grace: Yes, it is. I'm happy we can meet new people here.

Peter: I agree. It was nice meeting you both. I have to go now. Goodbye!

Alice & Grace: Goodbye, Peter! It was nice meeting you too!
